generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance JointAngleEncoderDrive1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder measuring angular displacement for Drive 1 of MABI MAX 100 robot, arranged to reduce backlash and positional uncertainty. Part of the sensing infrastructure providing precise positional feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointAngleEncoderDrive1DataTransmitted
]

instance JointAngleEncoderDrive2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder measuring angular displacement for Drive 2 of MABI MAX 100 robot, arranged to reduce backlash and positional uncertainty. Part of the sensing infrastructure providing precise positional feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointAngleEncoderDrive2DataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensorDrive1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor current sensor measuring electrical current output for Drive 1 of MABI MAX 100's permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM). Critical for dynamic modeling and torque estimation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDrive1DataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensorDrive2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor current sensor measuring electrical current output for Drive 2 of MABI MAX 100's permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM). Critical for dynamic modeling and torque estimation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDrive2DataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance JointAngleEncoderDrive1DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Time-series data of angular displacement from JointAngleEncoderDrive1, streamed via MQTT protocol for real-time processing. Represents positional feedback for Drive 1."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointAngleEncoderDrive1
]

instance JointAngleEncoderDrive2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Time-series data of angular displacement from JointAngleEncoderDrive2, streamed via MQTT protocol for real-time processing. Represents positional feedback for Drive 2."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointAngleEncoderDrive2
]

instance MotorCurrentDrive1DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Time-series data of motor current output from MotorCurrentSensorDrive1, streamed via MQTT protocol for real-time processing. Enables torque and dynamic analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensorDrive1
]

instance MotorCurrentDrive2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Time-series data of motor current output from MotorCurrentSensorDrive2, streamed via MQTT protocol for real-time processing. Enables torque and dynamic analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensorDrive2
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RobotGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model of MABI MAX 100 6-axis industrial manipulator defined using SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) and component-based ADLs (ACME/AADL). Captures structural relationships including components, connectors, and composition hierarchies."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RobotModelProcessingEnabler
]

instance RobotBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model of MABI MAX 100 implemented using SysML state machines (v1.7) or MontiArc Automaton, representing dynamic system behavior including motion control sequences and operational states."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RobotModelProcessingEnabler
]

instance RobotAnalyticalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model implemented in MathWorks Simulink for physics-based and control-oriented analysis of MABI MAX 100, including multi-physics interactions like joint friction and thermal effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RobotSimulationEnabler
]

instance JointAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series data of joint angles from all drives, processed for positional accuracy and used as input for kinematic and dynamic simulations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointAngleEncoderDrive1DataTransmitted, JointAngleEncoderDrive2DataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RobotModelProcessingEnabler, RobotSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series data of motor currents from all drives, processed for torque estimation and dynamic parameter identification."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDrive1DataTransmitted, MotorCurrentDrive2DataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RobotModelProcessingEnabler, RobotSimulationEnabler
]

instance MultiPhysicsSimulationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived data from multi-physics simulations (friction, thermal effects) executed in Simulink, used for continuous model identification and parameter updates."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RobotSimulationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RobotModelProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component handling SysML-based model management (geometric and behavioral) and data aggregation from MQTT streams. Implements model relationships via ADL interfaces (AADL/SysML IBDs)."
    DTDFVocab:enables RobotStructuralVisualizationService, RobotBehavioralAnalysisService
]

instance RobotSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine executing MathWorks Simulink models for real-time multi-physics analysis (joint friction, thermal effects) and continuous model identification. Enables data-driven parameter updates."
    DTDFVocab:enables RobotMultiPhysicsSimulationService, RobotPredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated data processing pipeline for MQTT-streamed sensor data, performing aggregation, abstraction, and validation before feeding into modeling/simulation components."
    DTDFVocab:enables RobotDataAcquisitionService
]

// C6: Services
instance RobotDataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive one-way data flow service collecting contextual data traces (joint angles, motor currents) from MABI MAX 100 via MQTT and Asset Administration Shells (AAS). Implements Digital Shadow functionality."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotContextualDataInsight
]

instance RobotStructuralVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "SysML-based visualization service representing MABI MAX 100's functional and logical architecture, including component interfaces, connectors, and composition relationships for stakeholder-specific perspectives."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotArchitectureInsight
]

instance RobotBehavioralAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Behavioral analysis service executing MontiArc Automaton or SysML state machines to model and analyze dynamic system behavior, including motion control sequences."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotBehavioralPatternInsight
]

instance RobotMultiPhysicsSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-physics simulation service performing real-time identification of joint friction, thermal effects, and dynamic parameters using Simulink-based analytical models."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotDynamicParameterInsight, RobotThermalEffectInsight
    DTDFVocab:enables RobotPredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance RobotPredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance service leveraging continuous model identification and multi-physics simulations to assess component health and operational efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotMaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RobotContextualDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data insight including joint angles, motor currents, and environmental conditions for real-time operational monitoring."
]

instance RobotArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural insight into MABI MAX 100's functional and logical architecture, supporting stakeholder-specific analysis (engineers, operators)."
]

instance RobotBehavioralPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified behavioral patterns and motion control sequences derived from state machine analysis and simulation."
]

instance RobotDynamicParameterInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuously identified dynamic parameters including joint friction coefficients and torque characteristics for all drives."
]

instance RobotThermalEffectInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Thermal effect analysis results from multi-physics simulations, including temperature distribution and heat dissipation patterns."
]

instance RobotMaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated maintenance recommendations based on predictive analysis of component health and operational efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "High-precision manufacturing environment hosting MABI MAX 100 6-axis industrial manipulator with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
    base:contains MabiMax100RobotComponent, Siemens840DControllerComponent
]

instance MabiMax100RobotComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "6-axis industrial manipulator (MABI MAX 100) with permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSM) and gear transmission for torque amplification. Operates in high-precision manufacturing tasks."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance Siemens840DControllerComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller managing motion control and operational sequences for MABI MAX 100 robot."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow system is explicitly defined as a **passive entity** that **neither operates autonomously nor pushes data back to the physical system**, distinguishing it from a Digital Twin. This means **no control flows, command types, or validation/authentication mechanisms** are present in this system. Consequently, there are **no feedback systems, conflict resolution mechanisms, or command execution confirmations** described in the document."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) constellation in this context is implicitly defined by the **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) framework**, which orchestrates the system through **multi-layered abstraction models** (requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers). The **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like ACME, AADL, or MontiArc provide the foundational **component integration** by defining systems from a **component & connector perspective**, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral descriptions are further refined using **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while **simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods)** and **general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java)** handle detailed dynamic behavior. The **system-wide coordination** is enabled by integrating these heterogeneous models and tools, ensuring alignment across stakeholders and perspectives. The document does not specify explicit orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven) but emphasizes the need for **active components** (as opposed to passive digital shadows) to interpret and process data flows, implying a **real-time or near-real-time orchestration** mechanism for DTs."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow system utilizes **MQTT protocol** for streaming sensor time series data from the physical object to the digital representation. While the document does not specify **network topology, bandwidth/latency requirements, or security protocols**, the use of MQTT implies a lightweight, publish-subscribe architecture suitable for IoT/edge environments. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not detailed, nor is the broader network infrastructure (e.g., cloud/edge integration) described."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates a **safety cell** for the robotic arm (MABI Max-100-2-25-P) that restricts its operational workspace to a predefined range of [-90°, 90°] to prevent physical collisions or hazards. The system leverages the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework (as referenced in [46], [47], and [50]) to standardize data representation and exchange, though the documents do not specify explicit cybersecurity protocols or encryption mechanisms for data transmission. Data streaming is facilitated via **MQTT** (as noted in [52]), a lightweight publish-subscribe protocol, but no details on authentication, authorization, or secure communication (e.g., TLS/SSL) are provided. The documents do not mention **fail-safe mechanisms**, **risk assessment methodologies**, or **safety-critical system validations** beyond the physical workspace restriction."
]
